Call Script
Follow each phase in order. Scroll through the script during your call.
Blue = Read to Client Yellow = Don't Read (Internal Notes) Teal = Optional Red = Important / Warning Purple = Pro Tips
OPEN ~30 sec ▶
West Coast Enrollment, this is ________! How can I help you?
Are you calling in regard to the debt hardship program to eliminate your unsecured debt?
If they ask: "What was this call for? Why did I get this call?"
Sure, not a problem. From what I can tell, it looks like your credit bureau — Equifax — labeled it as a financial hardship. They asked a law firm to reach out and offer assistance. That would be the purpose of my call today. So first question to you:
Back on script ↓
QUALIFY Under 2 min ▶
(Under 2 minutes: Type, Amount, Income)
1. Great — and what type of debts are causing your hardship? Credit cards and loans, or is it something else? (Personal Loans? Collection Accounts? Payday Loans? Cash Advances? Vehicle Repossession?)
2. And do you owe more than $6,000 in total?
3. And are you currently employed or do you have a source of household income? (SSI, SSDI, Unemployment?)
IF "No" to income: How are you currently paying your bills?If they have zero income get off the phone.
Perfect — it sounds like you might be eligible for one of our short-term hardship programs that will be a significant help to you. It will only take a few minutes to see if we can get you qualified, and I just need some basic information. Does that sound good?
Great, let’s see if we can get you some help here.
Just to let you know all calls are recorded for quality and security assurance ok?
BASIC INFO ~2 min ▶
(Name, Contact Info, D.O.B.Email )
CRM: In Motion, go to Leads → top right, click New Lead.
CRM: Make sure you select Marketer = Horizon Firm.
Perfect, we can get started pulling up your file with:
4. The proper spelling of your first and last name. (Reiterate spelling of first and last name.)
5. In case we get disconnected, is this the best number to reach you at? (Confirm phone number.)
6. And for verification purposes, what is your date of birth? (Confirm DOB.)
7. What’s a good email address for you (Confirm Email.)
Perfect. The next step for us today is to pull up a copy of your accounts and balances to figure out how much you owe and what debts we can and cannot help with. Its a soft credit pull so it will not affect your score.
The way we search for your debts is simply with your name and address.
Have you been living at your current address for more than 3 months
Perfect, and what is your full street address? (Confirm Address.)
CRM: After you have entered that info, scroll down to the bottom and select Create Lead.
After you have entered that info scroll down to the bottom and select
PROBE FOR PAIN Build rapport ▶
(Build rapport, gain understanding, show compassion — but find their weakness)
I am going to put you on a brief hold while we pull up your accounts.
At this point you will be brought back to the lead screen and select the lead you just created and select it

Once that happens you will be brought to this screen and select the debt tab

DEBT DISCUSSION Creditor review ▶
(Pre-fight interview: Creditor names, balances, total)
CRM: At this point you will be at a screen that looks like this. This is where you review all debts to decide what's included and what's not. The system does a good job but you need to double-check.
CRM: The next step is to select the report where the arrow is pointed.
CRM: That will pull up the credit report in detail.
that will then pull up the credit report in detail, and it will look something like this
Great. So I was able to locate everything. It looks like at this time your credit score is a ________.
I don’t mention this to beat you up — I want to point out a silver lining here.
My clients that come to me with a high score already, we have to tread lightly to not cause any damage. In your case, since the damage is already done, we can just power straight through and knock the debts off the report — which is going to leave you debt-free with a high score a lot faster than most of my clients.
I want to go through these one at a time:
(Go over the name and balance of each debt. Be delicate — a client wants to know you are listening and understand their situation. Cover: Creditor name, Balance, Status, Delinquent marks, Utilization %.)
Click on the lead you created and will bring you to this screen
And while I am waiting for your debts to load:
8. Can you tell me about your current situation? Most importantly, what are you trying to accomplish?
9. How much are you currently paying on your debts per month right now? Minimum payments or more than the minimum?
10. Are you seeing the balances go down at all?
PROBING / COMMITMENT Goals & income ▶
(Break them down to build them back up)
Okay great — so it looks like you have ________ accounts in total, and those accounts have a combined balance of $________.
Before I submit for approval, I always like to ask my clients if there were any specific goals for the day. In many cases when I am speaking with a borrower, it’s because an event occurred that sparked their interest in becoming debt-free with a high score.
Anything specific? Are you working towards a purchase? A goal you are trying to achieve? Or is this more to recover and rebound?
And did you have a time frame you were trying to achieve this in? (When is your purchase/goal?)
Okay, and next I wanted to go over your income to see if there are any additional hardship benefits I could get you approved for.
How much are you currently bringing home per month after taxes?
Do you have any other hardships such as loss of income? Unexpected bills? Health problems? Or COVID?
And how about savings? Do you have any emergency savings? Or have you depleted your savings at this point?
(Convey sympathy, compassion and concern — wait for response.)
(Enter Hardship Description in CRM.)
UNDERWRITING 2-3 min hold ▶
Walk-on song: Pen, paper, hold, build tension
Okay perfect — that is all the information I need from you. The next step for me today is to submit your debts to my underwriting department for approval.
If you are approved for a debt resolution program, I will help you get enrolled today. If you are denied for any reason, I will let you know the exact reasons.
I am going to put you on a brief hold for 2–3 minutes while I wait for a response. If you could grab yourself a pen and paper — when I come back on the line we will go over all your approval details together. Does this sound good?
Okay perfect, just give me a few minutes here. I will be right back on the line with a response. Please hold!
Place on hold for 2–3 minutes.
Push Data To Doc Pros And Go Create the Program
CRM Step 1: In Motion, click the button to convert the lead to a customer and push the data to Doc Pros CRM.
CRM Step 2: After you select Enroll, you will be brought to another screen. Scroll all the way to the bottom and select Continue.
CRM Step 3: At the bottom of the screen, select Push Data & Finish. Then select OK on the confirmation popup.
CRM Step 4: Go to Doc Pros CRM and find the lead. Click the client name to open their record.
CRM Step 5: On the client screen, click the Enrollment tab.
CRM Step 6: Click Select Plan and choose "The Doc Pros 1". Then select how many months. Make sure the payment plan is more than $230/month (that is the minimum).
CRM Step 7: Adjust the number of months to make the payment attractive to the client. The payment needs to be cheaper than their current monthly payments.
CRM: Go to the Calculator in this script helper and add the numbers from the program snapshot to get Owe / Pay / Save.
CRM: It will then give you the Owe / Pay / Save result. Now get back on the phone with the client and pitch.
HOW IT WORKS Education ▶
(Shadow box — show them what we do)
Hello ________, are you still with me? Perfect — I have some great news for you! It looks like my underwriting department was able to get ALL of your debts approved for the program. Congratulations! Do you have your pen and paper?
Great! First, I wanted you to write down the name of the program you have been approved for:
Debt Validation Validate & Update ▶
I have 2 words for you to write down as bullet points, and then I will circle back to what they mean. The 1st word is “Validate” and the 2nd word is “Update.”
So I wanted to take a moment to explain that first word — “Validate” — because this is going to do the most work for you with the types of debt you have.
To properly explain Validation, I need to give you a brief education on how to legally collect a debt from a borrower. In this case, you are the borrower.
There is a law in place called the “Fair Credit Reporting Act.” This law outlines what a creditor can and cannot do, and how they must follow the laws in order to collect a debt from a borrower.
The most basic portion of this law states a company cannot just “say” you owe a debt — they have to be able to “prove” it.
The way they prove it is by having your date of birth, social security number, and the original signed contract.
Your original creditors obviously had this information, but what a lot of people don’t know is that when a debt is sold or transferred in any way, there are multiple privacy laws in place that prevent most of this information from being sold.
The obvious ones are your social — no one can sell your social for a profit — and YOU would know better than anyone that after it was sold, you never sat down and signed any sort of new paperwork, agreements, or contracts with the 3rd-party collector.
That’s 2 of the 3 major required laws that are broken.
THIS IS WHERE WE STEP IN!!
We are partners with the Doc Pros. Simply put, we are going to demand legal proof from these creditors, which they WILL NOT HAVE. We will also hit them with Cease and Desist letters to stop all collection attempts.
We are going to demand information we know for a fact they will not have. Once we are able to prove the debt to be invalid, it is deemed uncollectable, and we will request that the entire trade line is removed from your credit report in an expungement.
What this means is, all said and done, not only is the debt balance itself not owed, but the FULL TRADELINE and history of delinquency will be removed,
so that no one in the future can see you even had these debts to begin with,
let alone did not pay back the full balance.
PRO TIP — ALL ACCOUNTS ACTIVE (NOT DEFAULTED)
READ THIS FOR EVERYONE IN GOOD STANDING — Print and tape to wall.
Add this to the pitch:
Now — that example was based on collection accounts. The only difference between collection accounts and your debts is that yours are still with the original creditor, meaning they still have your original signed contract. Not a problem — there’s just 1 additional step our attorneys will start off with, which is called a Strategic Charge-Off.
Our attorneys will force the debt to transfer, which will cause the loss of information to occur. This will allow us to validate and expunge these accounts the exact same way as if they were collection accounts.
Point being — these will all be handled in the same manner: Name, Account Number, Late Marks, and Balance Removed. Leaving you debt-free with a high score!
The 2nd step is a “Credit Report Update.” This is way less labor-intensive, but it does carry its own weight. If any of the 30, 60, and 90+ day late marks are remaining past step one, we will request to remove them as well. This further clears the way for a higher credit score as a result.
These are the 2 major steps on your path to becoming completely debt-free with a high credit score.
So this is a true credit report RESET. Does that make sense?
Next, I have a few numbers for you to write down — and then I will circle back to what they mean.
(Have client write down Owe, Pay, and Save numbers.)
PITCH Numbers & requirements ▶
(Let’s get ready to rumble! Numbers and requirements.)
EXAMPLE:
Owe – $20,000
Pay – $7,000
Save – $13,000
Okay, so the first number is everything you owe combined with interest. You can label that “OWE.”
Now the good news is, because you have been approved for debt validation, you will only be required to pay back a total balance of that 2nd number. You can label that “PAY.”
When I say “total payback,” I mean TOTAL PAYBACK — that is everything included: fees, cost, legal retainer costs, and repayment — all rolled into that number. It is a FLAT FEE program with no interest or hidden fees.
When you subtract this from what you currently owe, that difference is your savings. This is the 3rd number. You can label that “SAVE.”
So, from the top going down: that is Owe, Pay, and Save. Do these 3 numbers make sense?
It also appears this is going to be significantly cheaper per month vs. trying to pay them individually. Next I wanted to go over your approved payment schedule. I am happy to report this is going to be a very short program for you. As you may already know, ON YOUR OWN, this could take you over 6–10 years to pay these debts back IN FULL — because of the crazy high interest and fees.
The good news is, for this program your payment would be __ for __ months
Can give them option later — Payment options (adjust per client):
Option A: 12 × $452.07 (__ month program)
Option B: 18 × $321.34 (__ month program)
So, in addition to that save amount, this will also save you 5–7 years of payments.
In this program, your 1 payment will cover ALL of your debts again this is a flat fee program.
So this obviously REPLACES any payment to any of these accounts. You will no longer be making payments to them.
SOFT LANDING Mail instructions ▶
(Bob and weave — distract with an easy task)
Perfect. Now there is only 1 requirement moving forward. As you can imagine, those exact same laws that prevent your creditors from selling your birthday and social security number also prevent us from redirecting your mail straight to us. So all that means is: when we get started, the creditor bills will be mailed to your mailbox. Your only task moving forward is pretty simple — when you receive these letters, all you have to do is take a picture and send it to us through email. Sound easy enough?
These collection and creditor letters that you will receive in your mailbox are your GOLDEN TICKET to becoming debt free and completing this program quickly and on schedule.
So AS SOON as you receive them — send them in to us via Email or Text, I will provide all of those contact numbers at the end of our call today.
COMMITMENT QUESTION Transition to close ▶
(Uppercut — let’s rock!)
Okay. So if the numbers make sense and this is something you want to proceed with, then it’s a pretty straightforward enrollment. The next step for us today is to complete your application, then we will structure your program and pick out whatever payment dates work best for you.
Then, most importantly, we will get your E-Sign documents generated and sent to you so that you can review and sign electronically. Sounds good? Let’s do it!
(Don’t forget their “Pain” and “Goals” from earlier in the call — this might be the ammo you need to cross this bridge.)
CLOSE Application & payment ▶
(Last round! Complete the file: Application, Schedule, Card, Docs.)
Great. So the address you gave me earlier in the call — ________ — this is your current address, correct? Okay, perfect. And I have your income listed as ________, current credit score ________, and goals for the day: (read back goal to keep them motivated.) Correct?
And I always like to ask my clients: when we call regarding your program, is there a preferred time of day for our customer service team to reach you? Morning, Afternoon, or Night?
Perfect. And later in the call I am going to send my contact info and program documents. What’s the best email for you?
(Subtle reassurance that there will be good communication.)
Perfect — so your application is complete. The next step for us today is to structure your program. Lets pick out a payment date that works for you.
Your monthly payment secures your legal protection and starts the work on your accounts immediately. These balances are increasing every day — what's the soonest you can get started? Tomorrow is the soonest we can start.

But generally when someone asks for that, it’s more of an affordability issue rather than a scheduling issue — so if you need me to stretch out the term slightly, I can — so we can get started sooner rather than later.

E-Sign Instructions
11. Open text message that says “Document Signature Requested.”
12. Click the checkbox that agrees to terms and conditions using an e-signature document, then click the green button that says “Begin Signing.”
13. Tap on the orange initial box on the right-hand side that says “Tap / Click to Place Initials.”
14. Draw signature, then draw initials at the bottom using your finger. At the bottom, tap the green button that says “Adopt and Confirm Signature.”
15. Tap on the rest of the orange boxes throughout the document. At the bottom, once done, tap the green button that says “Finish Document.”
Once they have signed agreement and you see it in your mailbox read:
Great I have it and say congratulations, I am required to read you these questions so they know I did my job correctly.

QC SCRIPT Verification ▶
1. You understand the payments you are making go towards your validation program and the monthly legal coverage — and not directly to pay your debts, correct?
2. You understand this is not a debt settlement program, but a Debt VALIDATION program, and the payments for your program do not go to an escrow account?
3. You understand that in the very rare case that you receive a summons — Resolve Law Group will connect you with a local attorney who will assist you in settling the account for you at no cost for their LEGAL SERVICES — and that you will be paying that settlement directly to the creditor, but again this will still be at a fraction of the cost of the particular account?
4. You understand that your FIRST payment in the program is scheduled for ________ (Date) in the amount of $________. If your first payment is not successful and you do not reschedule, the program and all services will be canceled?
5. Lastly, we will require that you send us all 3rd-party collection letters you receive directly — so any you already have, or anything new that shows up, send us a copy through email or text.
Other than that, we are completely done for the day. Customer Support will reach out to you from their direct number, 949-288-5830, in the next 1–2 days for a short “Welcome Call.” It has been such a pleasure to assist you. Never hesitate to reach out if you have any questions, comments, or concerns. Hope you have a great rest of your day! Talk to you soon!
Congratulations on your close! You're on the board, BEAST!

Common Rebuttals
Click any rebuttal to expand the response script
"You don't pay off my debt?" ▶
The payments you're making don't go directly to your creditors. Instead, they go toward your validation program and your monthly legal coverage.
That's important because this program is what does the work of legally disputing and eliminating your debts.
Think of it as a chain of command — your payments fund the program, and the program resolves the debt on your behalf.
This isn't a traditional debt settlement where you just throw money at balances. It's a structured legal process that disputes the debts and gets them resolved right away.
Your debts wouldn't just disappear on their own — it's because of this program, and that's exactly what you're paying for.
"I don't feel right about not paying my debts" ▶
I completely understand and if you can pay your debts off, you absolutely should. But let's look at the numbers. How long have you been paying on these?
How much do you pay each month? (Multiply the payment times the number of months they have been paying) That's $XXXX you have paid already.
If more than the balance: "You have fulfilled your obligation so why is it right for the credit card companies to keep taking your money when the amount borrowed has been paid back?"
If less: "These programs are designed for people in financial hardship."
These credit card companies will still collect what's owed through insurance and tax incentives when they write it off. They make money again when they sell it to a 3rd party collector.
The credit card companies will be fine. They will still be a multi-billion dollar companies with or without your debts.
Remember, this is your financial situation we're talking about. Not theirs.
"Will I be sued?" ▶
It typically doesn't get to that point when attorneys are involved.
However, if that happens, you'll have full legal representation and defense included.
The attorneys will respond on your behalf, file all necessary paperwork, fedex any appropriate documents, and if necessary litigate on your behalf.
What happens if debt validation doesn't work?
Even in the rare case we have to settle an account, the law firm will make sure it's still completely removed from your credit report.
What happens in a lawsuit?
The Negotiation can start pre-emptively to avoid it from happening, with our law firm which is an attorney's office.
Hot Button Phrases:
Every one of your creditors get a notice of disputes.
The objective is to get you back to lendability and to remove those accounts as if they never existed!
With this Program there is 0 interest.
If any of the credits call you or break any regulations, the law firm can actually sue them and in some cases has won clients upwards of $5,000+.
"How do I know this will actually work?" ▶
We only take on accounts that we have had success with for over 10 years — for example if you had a secured debt or certain creditors or credit unions — we would NOT enroll in it.
We only enroll accounts this WORKS for.
Also — you're on a monthly plan — each month that goes by (AND you are sending in collection letters) you will see the proof in the pudding — mail hitting your mailbox from creditors, 3rd parties, credit bureaus proving we are doing the work we promise to do.
"This sounds too good to be true" ▶
You probably haven't heard of this program before because you haven't been in this situation before.
However, there are many consumer protection laws in place, such as the Fair Debt Collections Practices Act, the Fair Credit Billing Act, the Fair Credit Reporting Act, and most recently the Credit Card Act of 2009.
Do you remember when the banking crisis occurred? That was when more legislation was enacted to protect consumers like you from predatory lending and holding the creditors and banks accountable for their communication with you, keeping accurate records, and reporting correctly to the credit bureaus, which they misreport 80% of the time.
(The Doc Pros) and the law firm specializes in these laws, successfully disputing and resolving debts. This outcome typically results with the tradeline removed from your credit reports, like it never happened.
"I'm maxed out but I'm scared to fall behind" ▶
I completely understand your concern — nobody wants to fall behind. But here's the part nobody tells you:
Right now you're already paying as if you're behind — just without getting the benefit of the reset.
You're over 80–100% utilization. Your minimum payments barely touch principal. Your score is stuck or dropping. And you're one emergency away from a real default.
If what you're doing was working, the balances would be going down. But the interest keeps resetting every month. So clients in your situation end up stuck for 5–12 years paying minimums and never getting ahead.
Allowing accounts to move to collections doesn't "hurt you more" — it actually creates leverage. When it's charged off or with a 3rd-party, the burden shifts, paperwork changes hands, chain-of-title gets sloppy, and that's where disputes work in your favor. This is why the biggest improvements for clients happen after that transition — not before.
Your score can recover much faster with a structured dispute program than by paying it down. The balances get challenged, the negative accounts can be deleted, utilization drops, score rebounds, and you get back into lending territory quicker. It's not the late payments that tank people long-term — it's the years of maxed-out cards.
The temporary dip is exactly what unlocks the long-term recovery. You're choosing between a short, controlled step back — or dragging the problem out for another 5–10 years. One gets you back to a 650–700 range faster, the other keeps you stuck.
Short Version (for live calls):
"I get it — nobody likes missing payments. But right now, you're maxed out, your score is already suppressed, and the banks aren't giving you credit anyway. What the program does is turn the situation into an advantage by challenging the debt once it moves out of the original creditor's hands. This is the fastest way to reset your utilization, remove the negative accounts, and get you back to borrowing power. Staying current keeps you stressed for years — strategically stepping back gets you ahead faster."
"I'm not interested in a program" ▶
Totally fair. But this is like nothing you've ever heard before. Give me 5 minutes, and I think you'll agree. Fair enough?
"Creditors are going to call me" ▶
Yeah, they're gonna call. Good news — the law firm will instruct you exactly how to handle those calls. Either they'll stop the calls, or if they're violating your rights, they'll sue on your behalf at no cost to you. Sound good?
"I just need a loan" (Loan Rebuttal) ▶
Short, direct explanation (client-friendly):
I get why a loan sounds like the solution — on the surface it feels like it fixes everything. The problem is, with your current credit score and utilization, a loan actually doesn't solve the core issue and often makes things worse.
The logic (simple + undeniable):
Right now your credit is being held down by high utilization and negative account activity. A loan doesn't remove those — it just reshuffles the debt.
In most cases: the interest rate is high, the payment is higher than what you're already doing, your utilization stays elevated, and your score barely moves, if it moves at all.
The killer line (use this):
A loan helps people with good credit get better terms — it doesn't fix damaged credit.
Utilization reality check (very important):
Even if you got approved today, lenders don't look at intent — they look at risk. High utilization tells the system you're already stretched, so adding another loan just flags more risk.
That's why people take loans and six months later ask, 'Why didn't my score go up?'
Future-pacing consequence (NEPQ style):
If nothing changes on the credit side and you just add another payment, how long do you think it takes before you're right back in the same position — just with more debt and less flexibility?
(pause — let them answer)
Soft pivot to the real solution:
The reason lenders say no right now isn't because you don't want to pay — it's because the credit profile itself needs repair first.
Once utilization is corrected and negative accounts are addressed, then a loan actually makes sense — because it's cheaper, easier to approve, and actually helps your score.
Analogies (pick the one that fits):
Best overall: "Using a loan to fix bad credit is like putting premium gas in a car with a check-engine light on — it doesn't fix the problem, it just lets you drive a little longer before it breaks down again."
Street-level: "A loan on top of high utilization is like stacking another box on an already overloaded shelf — it doesn't strengthen it, it just makes the collapse happen faster."
Financial-minded: "A loan doesn't repair credit damage — it just changes the shape of the debt. Lenders don't reward that."
Emotional impact: "Taking a loan with high utilization is like taking painkillers for a broken bone — the pain feels better, but the injury never heals."
With pushback: "If loans fixed bad credit, lenders wouldn't care about utilization — but that's the first thing they look at."
How Is This Going To Affect My Credit?
Use the right version depending on the client's account status
Accounts Current but High Utilization ▶
That's a really good question — and I totally get why you'd ask that. Your credit is already being dragged down mainly by the high balances, not by missed payments. Utilization alone can drop a score a hundred points or more, so even though you're current, lenders still see you as high-risk right now.
What this program does is it helps you stop the bleeding — because if you keep paying minimums, your utilization never drops, and your score just stays stuck or even drops more the longer it stays that way.
Once those accounts are legally disputed, validated, and resolved, we will request that the entire trade line is removed from your Credit Report in an expungement.
What this means is, after everything is all said and done, not only is the debt balance itself not owed, but the FULL TRADELINE and all of your history on that account is COMPLETELY WIPED from your credit report.
So this will get you back to lendability within 18 months or less with a high credit score! (Most of our clients end up in the mid 700's)
Behind on Everything / Low Score ▶
I completely understand your concern — but at this stage, your credit's already taken the hit. Those late payments and collections have already dropped your score, so continuing to pay on invalid or charged-off accounts won't rebuild it.
What actually helps now is removing the negative and rebuilding with new, positive data.
Our process is designed to legally dispute and invalidate those accounts that are hurting your score — and when they can't be validated, they get removed completely with a $0 balance. That's what helps open the door for rebuilding.
So instead of trying to fix credit on top of damaged debt, we clean up the foundation first — this helps get you back to lendability in the quickest way possible.
Quick Reinforcement Lines ▶
Credit at rock bottom: "You can't hurt what's already broken — but you can fix what's broken by forcing creditors to prove the debt is valid and removing inaccurate data."
Credit rebound: "Once those negative items are disputed and validated, the tradelines that don't belong or can't be proven are deleted — and that's when your score starts to bounce back."
Shift to action: "The only way out is through a structured, legal process — not by letting collectors keep reporting negative information and dragging it out another 3–7 years."
Reset button: "Think of it like a reset button. We're not just putting a Band-Aid on your credit — we're removing the infection that's been causing the score to stay low in the first place."
Sales Playbook
Quick reference guide & battle cards
Initial Contact Checklist▶
Stop payments/revoke phone consent – send all collection accounts over
Check if accounts are OLD – verify they have an email on file
Find out what days they get paid to tighten 1st payment date
Cross-Collateralization Script▶
I want to quickly cover something important that protects your money while you're in the program — it's called cross-collateralization.
Since your checking account and credit card are with the same bank, once that card becomes past due, the bank has the right to pull money directly from your account to pay the balance. They don't ask — they just sweep the funds.
Because we're strategically allowing the account to fall behind so the validation process can work, we can't have your income going into a bank that can grab it.
So the safe move is: Open a new checking at a different bank, move direct deposit and bills there, leave the old account mostly empty so there's nothing to sweep.
This simply protects your money while we do our job on the debt.
Debt Validation Timelines▶
Account TypeTimeline
Already in Collections90-120 days (if they send letters immediately)
Current Accounts90-120 days til charge off + 90 days more = 180-210 days total
Bottom line: 6-12 months is realistic to dispute/invalidate all accounts
Key Talk Tracks▶
"Toxic Integrity" Reframe: "You're walking around with a pocket full of plastic that you can't use. If you swiped it right now, it would be declined. 'Toxic integrity' can sometimes keep people from making smart decisions. 'Do the right thing' sometimes means doing the smart thing."
The Payment Problem: "It sounds like the issue isn't that you can't make payments... it's that the payments aren't solving the problem. Would you agree?"
"Renting Your Bad Credit": "If you're carrying $20,000 in debt with a 600 score, you're basically renting your non-lendable credit score for $____ a month. Why keep paying to stay stuck when you could invest in fixing it?"
Lendability: "By lendability, I mean being able to qualify for the best interest rates from reputable lenders — and staying away from anything predatory."
Show Them The Math: Monthly payment × months = money wasted. Example: $700/mo × 36 months = $25,200 thrown out the door!
Bullet Points: Why This Is Better Than Doing Nothing▶
If you are in a position where you're struggling with debt/maxed out and only making minimum payments… you're essentially "renting" your non-lendable credit score.
Break down the math: They have 15k in debt, pay about $400/mo in mins for 2 years = $9,600 and balance is STILL 15K. In another 2 years: $19,200 paid and still same position. That's not progress — that's lighting your hard-earned money on fire!
If you're using all your income just to stay current — which is already keeping you from being lendable — what would you do if an emergency came up? How would you pay for it? That's exactly what this program gives you — payment relief and the ability to start saving right away.
Why Paying Old Collections Can Backfire▶
1. DLA Reset: Payment may reset the Date of Last Activity, making the account appear more recent. Newer negatives weigh more heavily.
2. No Immediate Score Improvement: Paid collections stay on report for 7 years. Many models treat paid and unpaid similarly.
3. Statute of Limitations Risk: Paying can restart the statute, allowing creditors to sue again.
4. Cash Flow Strain: Lump sum payments reduce available funds for essentials.
5. Neglecting Current Obligations: Focusing on old collections could divert resources from current bills.
Loan Denial Transition Scripts▶
Pre-Let Down:
So we're just going to take a look at your loan options. My only concern is that you're currently at [___]% credit utilization — you're using almost all your available credit, and that's really pulling your score down. Right now your score is at [___]. My concern is you may not qualify for an interest rate that's really going to help you. But don't be discouraged — because of your situation, this qualifies as a financial hardship. We have a hardship program that might be more aligned with your goals.
When Offers Are No Good:
Unfortunately, the offers didn't come in as we had hoped. So what I'm going to do is tell you about some options that I think are going to work really well for you. Sound good?
Why Loans Don't Fix The Problem:
If you're already behind on unsecured debt and it's hitting your credit, another loan just adds fuel to the fire. It usually comes with high interest and short terms. What you need isn't more debt — it's a way to freeze the damage, stop collections, and actually repair your credit.
Program Explanation Snippets▶
What The Program Does:
Goal: Get you back to lendability within 18-24 months and remove those accounts as if they never existed. After program completion, accounts show no derogatory remarks whatsoever.
Key Benefits:
Full service legal approach. Saving you significant money and interest. Taking over all communications with creditors/collectors. Single lower monthly payment that is interest-free. Attorneys stay with you throughout the entire process.
Why Creditors Can't Validate:
"Think of how many mergers, buyouts, and system changes these banks have been through. Getting the original signed agreements, interest calculations, disclosures, terms and conditions, notices of late fees — it's like looking for a needle on a 10,000-acre farm. They usually respond, but it's almost always incomplete."
Discovery & Pain-Finding Questions▶
Core Discovery:
Do you plan on using your credit for any major purchase in the next 12-24 months?
How long have you been carrying these balances for?
Are you expecting any significant increase in your income over the next 2 years?
What options have you looked into to get the debt resolved?
Do you have any money in savings at this time?
(If no savings) When was the last time you were able to put money in savings? Would you say the debt is preventing that?
(If 40+) Do you have any money in retirement?
Pain-Finding (Emotional Sale):
How long has this been going on?
Are you at least able to keep up with basic expenses?
What are you going to do if it doesn't get better?
How is it affecting your family? How is it affecting your health?
Soft Openers: "Just out of curiosity..." / "Help me understand..." / "Can you walk me through..." / "How do you see that affecting..." / "What happens if nothing changes?" / "Would it be helpful if..."
Debt Settlement vs. Debt Validation
Training Module — Help reps clearly explain how Debt Validation works, why it's legally stronger and financially safer than traditional settlement, and how it protects credit and cash flow better.

1. The Big Picture: Both Are About Getting Out of Debt

Debt Settlement:
Negotiates balances after a charge-off.
You stop paying creditors → accounts go late → credit score tanks.
A negotiator tries to settle accounts for less than owed (usually 40–60%).
You still pay income tax on forgiven amounts.
Credit remains damaged for years.
Debt Validation / Dispute:
Uses federal consumer protection laws (FDCPA, FCRA, FCBA, etc.) to force collectors to prove a debt is valid and legally collectible.
If they cannot produce required documentation, the debt must be deleted or closed — not settled.
Focuses on disputing legality, not negotiating a payoff.
You keep more cash and protect your credit from new damage.

2. Financial Comparison (Average Case)

CategoryDebt SettlementDebt Validation
Total Savings40–50% off balances (but pay taxes on forgiven debt) Plus 25% to the settlement company (after all costs they pay back up to 100% of the debt they owed)Often 60–80% savings, no tax liability
Monthly PaymentHigh, based on negotiated settlementsLow, based on legal service plan
Credit ImpactSevere – late payments and settlements reportedMinimal to moderate – disputed accounts may be removed
Legal ProtectionNone (can still be sued)Strong – uses legal team and dispute process
Timeline36–48 months average6–18 months average depending on accounts

3. Credit Restoration Advantage

Debt Settlement:
Leaves "settled for less than owed" marks.
Score usually drops 100–200 points.
Must rebuild from scratch after program.
Debt Validation:
When debts are invalidated, the negative trade lines are disputed for removal.
Validated debts can be handled legally without settlements.
Goal: restore credit to 680–720+ within program completion.

4. Real-World Example

A client owes $20,000 across five credit cards.

Debt Settlement: pays ~$12,000–$14,000 + taxes → ends up costing ~$15,000 total, also 25% fees to the settlement company it could be up to $20,000 all in!

Debt Validation: legal team disputes each account. Three are deleted, let's say two are verified and negotiated for $4,000 → total ~$6,000-$8,000 total payback.

Savings: $9,000 difference + credit rebound in under a year.

5. Talking Points for Reps

"We don't negotiate your debt — we challenge it legally."
"Our goal isn't to settle — it's to remove what's not legally collectible."
"You keep more money in your pocket and restore your credit faster."
"Debt settlement focuses on paying; we focus on protecting."
"We help you get your financial rights enforced under federal law."

6. Training Exercise — 60-Second Explanation

Have each rep practice explaining the difference in under 60 seconds, using this framework:
"Debt settlement tries to cut deals with creditors, but it wrecks your credit and you still pay taxes on the forgiven amount. Debt validation uses federal law to force collectors to prove a debt's legality. If they can't, it must be removed — not settled. That's how our clients keep more money and rebuild credit faster."
Collections — Why It Works
Walk the client through the collection lifecycle with certainty
(Absolute Certainty) Let's take your CREDITOR account for example. Soon after this account reaches late or non-payment status, CREDITOR is going to sell your account to a collections company. In doing so CREDITOR gets a tax break by writing off your debt as "uncollectable" and "charges it off" while at the same time selling it to a collections company.
(Absolute Certainty) CREDITOR takes their money, is now out of the picture and has sold your account for pennies on the dollar to the debt collector. This debt collector is now seeking to collect the full amount from you! Pretty good business deal for them especially when you consider the only thing they really bought was paper!
(Absolute Certainty) When they purchase your debt, they simply receive a spreadsheet with your contact info, account number and how much you owe. Their only goal is to make as much money off you as possible. Once the collections company has your info, you'll get a letter in the mail stating that they took over your debt and they are trying to collect on behalf of CREDITOR.
(Absolute Certainty) We know that all of the time that is not true because CREDITOR has already sold this debt to them, made their money, and is out of the picture. Once you get that letter, you simply forward it to us and allow our team to handle the dispute process on your behalf from A to Z.
(Reasonable Man) Pretty straightforward right?
WAIT FOR AN ANSWER — BE PREPARED TO FILL IN ANY GAPS HERE.
NEPQ Cancellation Saves
Neuro-Emotional Persuasion Questioning — use these to save cancellations
NEPQ Training Video — Jeremy Miner
What is NEPQ? NEPQ stands for Neuro-Emotional Persuasion Questioning — a sales methodology developed by Jeremy Miner. It's a modern, psychology-based selling framework built around asking the right questions rather than pitching or pushing.
Key principles: Ask, don't tell — questions lead prospects to recognize their own pain. Neuro-emotional triggers — tap into emotional drivers behind buying decisions. Disarm defensiveness — avoids high-pressure tactics. Status quo disruption — creates urgency without aggression.
Question framework: 1) Connection questions — Build rapport. 2) Situation questions — Understand where they are now. 3) Problem awareness — Help them articulate pain. 4) Consequence questions — Explore what happens if nothing changes. 5) Solution questions — Guide them to envision a better outcome. 6) Commitment questions — Natural close based on their own reasoning.
1. Revisit Emotional Pain (Current vs. Future State)▶
"Out of curiosity, what was the main reason you enrolled in the first place? Was it more about wanting to stop the creditor pressure, or fixing your credit so you can borrow again?"
Let them respond
"Got it. So if you were to cancel now, do you feel like that would move you closer to the goal you originally had — or back to where things were before?"
2. Problem Awareness + Fear of Future Pain▶
"I get it — making changes like this can feel risky. But just to clarify, if those accounts stay on your credit the way they are now… do you think they'll improve on their own — or possibly get worse over time?"
Let them acknowledge the reality
"Exactly. That's actually why people with lower credit scores benefit the most from this program — it gives you a legal way to clean up your report and finally move forward."
3. Consequences of Doing Nothing▶
If Current:
"Let me ask you something — if nothing changes and those high balances stay where they are for the next 12 to 24 months, what does that mean for your credit and ability to qualify for anything new?"
Let them reflect
"Exactly. That's why people in your position benefit from this kind of legal strategy."
If Behind:
"If nothing changes, and those collections stay on your report for the next 12–24 months, what does that mean for you financially?"
"That's what we're trying to prevent by legally disputing and removing them now, instead of letting the damage drag out."
4. Reframe Cancellation as Losing Progress▶
"You've already taken the first step most people never take — and your file is already in motion. If we stop now, we lose the progress, and you're right back where you started — except now with another delay. Is that something you're willing to risk just yet?"
5. Rebuild Trust + Show Partnership▶
"I totally respect your concern, and that tells me you're serious about your credit. But that's why we're not like traditional debt settlement — we don't negotiate; we use consumer law to challenge the debt altogether. Would it be okay if I walk you through how this actually protects your credit instead of hurting it?"

Consumer Protection Laws
Reference these when educating clients
LawWhat It Does
FDCPAConsumers can ask for debt collector ID and proper debt validation
FCRAConsumers can review and dispute inaccurate credit reports
Credit Card Act 2009Establishes transparent and fair credit practices
TILARequires standardized disclosure of loan terms and costs
Right to Financial PrivacyProtects confidentiality of financial records
Unfair/Deceptive PracticesProhibits deceptive debt collection activities
Fair Credit Billing ActRequires creditor identity disclosure before extending credit
Equal Credit OpportunityForbids discrimination in credit extension

What We Request From Creditors (Under FCRA & TILA)

1. Original signed loan agreement/promissory note
2. Complete itemized payment history with all charges, fees, interest, credits
3. Breakdown of principal, interest rate, and any term changes
4. Proof of legal right to collect (chain of ownership if sold)
5. Copies of all notices/disclosures provided under federal lending laws
"Think of how many mergers, buyouts, and system changes these banks have been through. Getting the original signed agreements, interest calculations, disclosures, terms and conditions, notices of late fees — it's like looking for a needle on a 10,000-acre farm. They usually respond, but it's almost always incomplete."

Debt Type Cheat Sheet & Codes

Quick reference for reading credit report codes and deciding what to enroll.

Credit Report Codes

INST — INSTALLMENT LOAN — Always ask: What is this? What is it SECURED TO? — if anything
OPEN — Treat same as INST — always ask: What is this? What is it SECURED TO?
AUTO — Car must be REPO'd or completely totaled — watch for Joint borrowers attached!
REV — Revolving Credit Card / Line of Credit — always good but watch for Authorized User!
COLL — Collection account — always good
MTG / EDU — Mortgage and Student Loan — DON'T enroll EVER

Borrower Type Codes

B | B — Borrower / Borrower = just the client's debt. Good to go.
J | B — JOINT Borrower = there is a joint borrower attached to the debt. "Who is it?" Spouse or family member — they must be on board for the "Storm" (their credit will be affected too!). If joint borrower is on board, include their Name + Number + DOB in Co-Applicant Section. ONLY if the account is already IN Collections — no worries if client doesn't want to include them.
If spouse/family member wants to be included — ask if they have any Personal Debt. Include it to increase fees — and they also get the CREDIT UPDATE step included for FREE!
A | B — Client is just an AUTHORIZED USER on this credit card — do NOT include, not theirs!

Installment Loan Rules

INST on Credit Report — "WHAT IS THIS DEBT?" "WHAT IS IT SECURED TO?" A lot of these ARE secured — to a car, home improvement loan, tractor, etc. — we cannot include. But SOME are unsecured. Always ask: WHAT IS IT / SECURED TO?

Credit Union Rules

A) A credit card from a credit union is GOOD — ALL DAY, RUN IT
B) A personal loan or installment loan from a credit union is BAD — Exclude

Secured vs Unsecured

You ALWAYS HAVE TO ASK — don't rely on what it says on the credit report. It is sometimes listed as UNSECURED but is actually SECURED. You have to ask! Never ever try to push through a debt that is secured to an asset — client will be SUED, ASSET taken, and client will be pissed off you lied to them.
Do NOT Enroll "CHIME" or Secured REV's or Credit Builders

AI Practice — Choose a Prospect
Pick a persona to practice with. Start easy and work your way up. Click "Start" once the call loads, then speak your opening line.
M
Maria — Easy Callback
EASY
Got the RVM and is calling back. Doesn't know what the program is but is open to hearing about it. Answers questions, gives info when asked. Great for practicing your opening and qualification flow.
J
James — Curious Caller
EASY
Got the RVM about being approved for a debt hardship program. Doesn't know what it is but is curious. Keeps asking "how does it work?" but doesn't fight you. Good for practicing your opening, qualification, and Debt Validation explanation.
L
Linda — Friend Told Her
EASY
A friend mentioned the program to her but she doesn't really know what it is. She's open but still needs you to explain exactly what debt validation is and how the program works. Practice your Owe/Pay/Save pitch and walking someone through the full process.
K
Kevin — Credit Worrier
MEDIUM
Got the RVM and called back. Doesn't know what the program is yet. Once you start explaining, he gets hung up on his credit score dropping. Keeps circling back to "what happens to my credit?" Practice the credit impact rebuttal and soft landing.
D
Diane — Spouse Blocker
MEDIUM
Got the RVM and called back. Doesn't know what the program is. Once you explain it she's interested but keeps saying "I need to talk to my husband first." Uses the spouse as a shield. Practice commitment questions and creating urgency without being pushy.
T
Tony — Loan Seeker
MEDIUM
Got the RVM and called back thinking this is a loan or consolidation program. Doesn't know what debt validation is. Keeps asking "can I just get a loan?" Practice redirecting away from the loan objection and explaining what the program actually does.
R
Rick — The Skeptic
HARD
Got the RVM and called back but thinks it's a scam. Doesn't know what the program is and doesn't trust it. "Too good to be true." Challenges everything, won't give info easily. Practice handling trust objections and the "how do I know this works?" rebuttal.
A
Angela — Hostile Callback
HARD
Got the RVM and is calling back angry she was contacted. Doesn't know what the program is and doesn't care. Defensive, emotional, throws every objection. Won't pay, scared of being sued, doesn't trust anyone. The full gauntlet — every rebuttal in the book.
Tips for Practice
  • Click Start and speak your opening line as if you just answered a callback
  • Start with Easy prospects to nail the script flow, then move to Medium and Hard
  • Work through all phases: Qualify → Probe → Debt Discussion → Pitch → Close
  • Use the Owe / Pay / Save structure when presenting numbers
  • Session auto-ends at 15 minutes — keep it tight like a real call
Practice Call
